evals.prompt: Our Vite React app already uses @fluentui/react-components v9 and has no application styling convention yet. Build the provider and theme boundary for a branded light/dark application shell. Explain where the provider belongs and how feature components should consume color and spacing.
assertions: Uses the public v9 FluentProvider and a supported theme object rather than v8 ThemeProvider or copied CSS values.
Maps one client brand source through a theme adapter to complete light and dark Fluent themes, with namespaced application variables only for missing semantics.
Uses a CSS Module with semantic Fluent CSS variables for feature styles and avoids hard-coded theme or palette colors.
Places the provider at an intentional application or themed-subtree boundary and includes theme-switch validation.

evals.prompt: Implement a reusable StorageUsageMeter in a Fluent UI React v9 account page. It receives a runtime percentage from 0 to 100, has normal, warning, and critical states, supports a client light/dark theme, and must remain correct in RTL and forced-colors mode. The repository has no styling convention yet.
assertions: Keeps static presentation in a CSS Module and binds only documented root or slot classes from TSX.
Represents normal, warning, and critical as classes, data attributes, or component state rather than dynamic inline colors.

Lets the public ProgressBar value prop own the continuous percentage; if no public API owns a future continuous value, permits only a CSS custom property. Components, stories, tests, and fixtures contain no ordinary style-prop declarations.
Uses semantic Fluent variables, logical CSS properties, and forced-colors behavior without Tailwind or application makeStyles.

evals.prompt: Create a reusable TypeScript React formatting toolbar with icon-only Bold and Italic actions in Fluent UI v9. It must work for keyboard and screen-reader users, preserve consumer className and event handlers, and avoid private DOM selectors.
assertions: Uses public Toolbar and ToolbarToggleButton controls so toggle state and roving focus follow the Fluent toolbar contract.
Provides an accessible toolbar name and accessible names for icon-only actions.
Preserves consumer className and event behavior through public props or slots without private DOM selectors.
Tests ArrowLeft and ArrowRight focus movement plus Enter or Space activation through role/name queries rather than class-name assertions.

evals.prompt: A teammate wants to use useButton_unstable and renderButton_unstable for every app Button wrapper because it looks more flexible. Review the proposal and design a safer wrapper strategy that still supports an icon, loading state, className, and click handling.
assertions: Rejects routine use of _unstable state/render APIs when public Button props and slots satisfy the wrapper.
Preserves supported consumer props and handlers through a thin domain wrapper.
Allows unstable recomposition only behind an isolated adapter with an explicit unmet requirement and upgrade tests.

evals.prompt: Our Fluent UI v9 card wrapper mixes makeStyles, Tailwind utilities, inline styles, and !important overrides. Dark mode and RTL now fail. Diagnose the ownership problem and propose a minimal style refactor that preserves consumer className.
assertions: Moves touched static application styles to the repository stylesheet convention, using a CSS Module when no convention exists, and does not add Tailwind utilities.

Uses Fluent semantic CSS variables and logical properties; prefers an owning public prop before limiting any necessary inline style to a continuous runtime custom property, and names any retained Griffel capability.
Preserves consumer className and public slot classes with deliberate composition while removing broad specificity escalation.
Includes dark theme, RTL, and forced-colors checks.

evals.prompt: Plan the first migration slice for a React app using office-ui-fabric-react and @fluentui/react v8. The slice contains a command bar, dialog, custom mergeStyleSets styles, and tests that query Fabric class names. Keep the app shippable during migration.
assertions: Inventories package generations, providers, styling, DOM-coupled tests, and component behavior before replacement.
Migrates one coherent slice and maps behavior plus accessibility instead of mechanically renaming imports.
Moves legacy application styles to the repository stylesheet convention, using CSS Modules by default and retaining Griffel only for a documented capability that classes cannot safely preserve.
Moves tests to public semantics and defines bounded coexistence with an adapter deletion condition.
evals.escalation: Escalate when no public v9 component satisfies a critical interaction.

evals.prompt: Our Next.js App Router application uses @fluentui/react-components and flashes unstyled content on first load, then logs hydration class mismatches. Design the investigation and correction without assuming a copied SWC-plugin example is current.
assertions: Inspects installed Fluent, Griffel, React, and Next.js versions plus the current router mode before prescribing an API.
Uses request-safe renderer ownership and deterministic server style insertion through the verified App Router integration point.
Uses the installed public SSRProvider when required to keep Fluent-generated IDs deterministic.
Uses a one-shot didRenderRef guard or verified equivalent so streaming insertion does not emit duplicate style buckets.
Treats experimental compiler plugins as version-gated rather than universal and validates production hydration.
evals.escalation: Escalate when streaming or cache boundaries make renderer ownership ambiguous.

evals.prompt: Add three Fluent System icons to a TSX command surface. The app uses an older moduleResolution setting and one color icon loses meaning in Windows High Contrast Mode. Keep Fluent package decisions separate from TypeScript configuration ownership.
assertions: Uses atomic public icon imports and distinguishes decorative icons from accessible control names.
Verifies color or SVG behavior in forced colors and does not rely on color alone.
Hands module-resolution changes to martix-typescript instead of weakening types or switching to private imports.

evals.prompt: Integrate Fluent UI into a Vue 3 application. A blog post tells us to call provideFluentDesignSystem and copy React FluentProvider examples. We have @fluentui/web-components 3.x and use Vue SSR. Produce the correct integration plan and boundaries.
assertions: Uses @fluentui/web-components rather than React components, FluentProvider, or Griffel.
Rejects provideFluentDesignSystem and class .define() guesses in favor of installed v3 side-effect definition imports.
Configures Vue custom-element recognition for fluent-* tags.
Provides or points to a concrete HTMLElementTagNameMap or Vue GlobalComponents declaration for used Fluent elements.
Keeps registration client-safe and defines how SSR hydration will be tested.
Uses the verified setTheme utility with @fluentui/tokens rather than a React or removed provider model.

evals.prompt: We need an accessible themed line chart in a Fluent UI React dashboard. A proposal says Fluent UI has no chart package and mandates a third-party library. Review that claim and define a package-selection and accessibility plan.
assertions: Recognizes the official @fluentui/react-charts package and verifies its current status and capability.
Evaluates chart type, accessibility, bundle, and compatibility requirements before selecting a library.
Requires semantic descriptions or data alternatives and Fluent token integration for either package choice.

evals.prompt: Optimize a React product-card component that uses plain CSS Modules. It has no Fluent UI dependencies, tokens, Microsoft design requirements, or migration plans. Fix its unnecessary rerenders and image loading.
assertions: Recognizes that no Fluent UI implementation concern is present.
Hands the work to React performance and browser image guidance.
Does not introduce FluentProvider, Fluent packages, tokens, or Griffel.

evals.prompt: Our React 19 dashboard uses @fluentui/react-components v9. Build a branded light/dark FluentProvider boundary and refactor the hard-coded card colors to semantic tokens.
evals.expected_output: Activates martix-fluent-ui and routes to provider, theme, token, and React implementation guidance.

evals.prompt: fabric commandbar migration: we still have office-ui-fabric-react, mergeStyleSets and tests coupled to ms-Button. Plan one safe v9 slice.
evals.expected_output: Activates martix-fluent-ui and routes to the v8 or Fabric-to-v9 migration workflow.

evals.prompt: The Next.js App Router build flashes unstyled Fluent UI content and logs hydration class mismatches. Check Griffel renderer and server style insertion.
evals.expected_output: Activates martix-fluent-ui and routes to the Next.js and Griffel SSR ownership guidance.

evals.prompt: Create an accessible icon-only formatting toolbar from Fluent UI components in TSX, including keyboard and focus tests.
evals.expected_output: Activates martix-fluent-ui and routes to Fluent component composition, accessibility, focus, and test guidance.

evals.prompt: Should our wrapper use useButton_unstable/renderButton_unstable, or can Fluent v9 slots preserve icon, loading, click and className behavior?
evals.expected_output: Activates martix-fluent-ui and applies the public composition versus unstable API decision gate.

evals.prompt: Our makeStyles and Tailwind overrides fight in RTL and dark mode. Repair the Fluent token and Griffel ownership without private selectors.
evals.expected_output: Activates martix-fluent-ui and routes to CSS-first token ownership, bounded Griffel exceptions, RTL, theme, and override guidance.

evals.prompt: Use Fluent UI in Vue 3 SSR. We installed @fluentui/web-components 3.x, but the article we found uses provideFluentDesignSystem.
evals.expected_output: Activates martix-fluent-ui and routes to the narrow Vue 3 Fluent Web Components integration branch.

evals.prompt: Review @fluentui/react-charts for an accessible themed line chart before we commit to Recharts in our Fluent dashboard.
evals.expected_output: Activates martix-fluent-ui and routes to Fluent chart selection, theming, accessibility, and package verification guidance.

evals.prompt: Three @fluentui/react-icons imports fail in our TSX package and the color icon disappears in Windows High Contrast. Separate icon fixes from tsconfig ownership.
evals.expected_output: Activates martix-fluent-ui for icon and forced-colors guidance while handing TypeScript configuration ownership to martix-typescript.

evals.prompt: The Dialog looks right but Escape, initial focus and focus restore are broken after our custom Fluent slot rendering. Fix the behavior and tests.
evals.expected_output: Activates martix-fluent-ui and routes to Fluent Dialog composition, keyboard, focus, and behavior-test guidance.

evals.prompt: Optimize rerenders in a React product card built with CSS Modules. There are no @fluentui packages or Microsoft design requirements.
evals.expected_output: Does not activate martix-fluent-ui and hands the task to React performance guidance.

evals.prompt: Design a generic TypeScript polymorphic component API and publish declarations under NodeNext. It is not tied to any UI library.
evals.expected_output: Does not activate martix-fluent-ui and hands the task to martix-typescript.

evals.prompt: Create an SPFx Application Customizer, package the .sppkg, configure tenant-wide deployment and request Microsoft Graph permissions.
evals.expected_output: Does not activate martix-fluent-ui and hands the task to martix-sharepoint-spfx.

evals.prompt: Implement a FluentValidation AbstractValidator for a .NET request with async database rules and RuleSets.
evals.expected_output: Does not confuse FluentValidation with Fluent UI and hands the task to martix-fluentvalidation.

evals.prompt: Use Microsoft's Fluent 2 visual language to prepare a Figma mood board; no code or Fluent UI package is involved.
evals.expected_output: Does not activate martix-fluent-ui and routes the non-code design request to appropriate design guidance.

evals.prompt: Our Vue app uses Vuetify. Fix its Pinia store and route guards; do not change the design-system dependency.
evals.expected_output: Does not activate martix-fluent-ui and keeps the task with Vue, Pinia, and routing guidance.

evals.prompt: Configure Tailwind container queries and CSS cascade layers for a plain HTML marketing page.
evals.expected_output: Does not activate martix-fluent-ui and routes to Tailwind and CSS guidance.

evals.prompt: Run an accessibility audit of a native HTML checkout form that does not use React or Fluent components.
evals.expected_output: Does not activate martix-fluent-ui and routes to general web accessibility guidance.

evals.prompt: Upgrade React Testing Library and Vitest mocks in a Chakra UI application. Component behavior is unchanged.
evals.expected_output: Does not activate martix-fluent-ui and routes to React testing and Chakra UI guidance.

evals.prompt: Write a PowerShell function that exports Microsoft 365 audit logs in the Fluent Bit format.
evals.expected_output: Does not confuse Fluent Bit with Fluent UI and routes to PowerShell and logging guidance.
